title: lilium carniolicum subsp. ponticum
intro:
Lilium carniolicum subsp. ponticum, also known as the Dinaric Lily or Illyrian Lily, is a subspecies of the Lilium carniolicum species, a type of lily native to the Balkan Peninsula and parts of Central Europe.

Lilium carniolicum subsp. ponticum is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic effects. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, such as wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems, due to its health benefits in reducing inflammation, promoting healing, and exhibiting expectorant properties. The plant can be prepared into various herbal forms, including infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used in traditional medicine to harness its medicinal properties.
